title: Bloom and Big Bird | Dimensional
gist: Dimensional argues that fixed annual rebalancing schedules in cap-weighted indices cause style drift: the Russell 2000's largest constituent, Bloom Energy, has ballooned to an $81B market cap (1.82% of the index) ahead of the June rebalance, leaving small-cap index fund investors holding a stock that would rank 151st in the large-cap Russell 1000.

themes.theme: Index style drift from rigid rebalancing

insights.claim: Fixed, infrequent index rebalancing schedules cause cap-weighted indices to drift away from their stated style, leaving holdings far removed from the index's intended asset category until the next scheduled reconstitution.
insights.speaker: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Investors using index funds for precise factor exposure (e.g. small cap) can unknowingly hold off-mandate large caps for up to a year; daily/flexible implementation avoids this drag.
insights.quote: Irregular rebalancing often causes style drift in indices. Occasionally, this results in holdings that are far removed from the intended asset category.
insights.locator: Opening paragraph, 'Irregular rebalancing often causes style drift'

insights.claim: As of May 31, 2026, Bloom Energy is the largest constituent of the Russell 2000 Index with an $81 billion market capitalization, representing 1.82% of the small-cap index.
insights.speaker: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: A single $81B 'small cap' is a concentration and mislabeling risk for Russell 2000 trackers heading into the June rebalance.
insights.quote: The index's largest constituent, Bloom Energy, has a market cap of $81 billion. That accounts for 1.82% of the index
insights.locator: Paragraph on June rebalance / Bloom Energy size outlier

insights.claim: With an $81 billion market cap, Bloom Energy would rank as the 151st largest stock in the large-cap Russell 1000 Index, underscoring how far the Russell 2000's top holding has outgrown its small-cap mandate.
insights.speaker: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Quantifies the style-drift gap: a top-150 large cap sitting inside a small-cap benchmark distorts the factor exposure investors think they bought.
insights.quote: With an $81 billion market cap, Bloom Energy would be the 151st largest stock in the large cap Russell 1000 Index.
insights.locator: Footnote 1, market capitalization as of May 29, 2026

insights.claim: Index maintenance schedules can make it challenging for investors to capture the asset-class exposure they expect from index funds, because a lot can change in the year between scheduled reconstitutions.
insights.speaker: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Supports Dimensional's pitch for flexible, daily implementation over calendar-bound index tracking when precise exposure matters.
insights.quote: the constraints enforced by index maintenance schedules can make it challenging for investors to get what they're expecting out of index funds
insights.locator: Paragraph 'A lot can happen in a year'

source_meta.author: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
